task: I’m putting together a punch list for a rehab unit, and I want a simple way to make sure nothing gets signed off too early. What checkpoints would you use so I can catch defects, missing paperwork, or scope creep before I pay anyone?

turns.agent_response:
Use a **3-bucket signoff gate** so you don’t confuse a finished-looking unit with a truly closed job:

## 1) Physical defect check
Before any payment, verify:
- All punch items are actually complete
- No cosmetic cover-ups hiding defects
- Fixtures, appliances, doors, windows, HVAC, plumbing, electrical all function
- No leaks, stains, cracks, uneven finishes, missing trim, bad caulk, loose hardware
- Cleanliness: construction debris, dust, paint splatter, adhesive, trash removed
- Final walkthrough is done with lights on, water running, HVAC running, outlets tested

## 2) Paperwork / compliance check
Do not sign off until you have:
- Final invoice tied to approved scope
- Change orders in writing, with pricing
- Permits closed out, if applicable
- Lien waivers from contractor and subs
- Warranty info / manuals for equipment and appliances
- Photos of completed work, especially anything covered up
- Any inspection signoffs or required certificates

## 3) Scope creep / cost control check
Confirm:
- Every extra item was owner-approved before work was done
- No “we had to” charges without proof
- Labor/materials match the agreed scope
- Unit count, fixture count, and material quality match the bid
- No duplicate billing, vague line items, or surprise add-ons

## Simple payment rule
**No pay until all 3 buckets pass.**  
If one fails:
- **Physical defect** → hold payment until fixed
- **Paperwork/compliance** → hold payment until complete
- **Scope creep** → reject or renegotiate before paying

## Fast punch list format
For each item, track:
- Location
- Defect
- Priority
- Responsible party
- Due date
- Photo attached
- Verified complete by

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page rehab closeout checklist** you can use on site.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I’d tighten it up more. Too much of that still reads like generic contractor advice.

What I actually use is a hard gate by phase:

1) **Work complete in the field**
- Walk every room with the scope in hand
- Verify fixtures, appliances, trim, paint, flooring, doors, windows, electrical, plumbing, HVAC
- Test everything live, not eyeballed
- No touch-up left on a promise

2) **Hidden-work verification**
- Anything covered up gets photo proof before close-in
- No drywall closed without plumbing/electrical/signoff photos if that matters
- If there was a change behind the wall, I want it documented

3) **Code / permit / inspection closeout**
- Final inspection passed, not “scheduled”
- Permit card closed if the job required it
- Any correction notices resolved in writing

4) **Money control**
- Final invoice matches approved scope exactly
- Every change order signed before the work, not after
- Lien waivers from GC and subs
- No release of retainage until the job is actually closed

5) **Scope creep check**
- Compare bid vs. actual by line item
- Watch for junk like “misc labor,” “extra trip,” “material adjustment” with no backup
- If it wasn’t approved, it doesn’t get paid

6) **Closeout package**
- Warranty docs
- Manuals
- Serial numbers if applicable
- Before/after photos
- Final walkthrough signoff

My rule is simple: **if it can’t be documented, it didn’t happen.**

If you want, I can turn this into a tighter **pay application checklist** or a **one-page rehab closeout form** with yes/no boxes.

turns.agent_response:
That’s the right structure. I’d tighten it into a **6-gate closeout** with a hard rule at the end:

## Rehab closeout gates

### 1) Field complete
- Walk every room against the scope
- Test all fixtures, appliances, doors, windows, outlets, plumbing, HVAC
- No “minor touch-up” left open
- No item marked done unless it works

### 2) Hidden work documented
- Photos before drywall / close-in
- Photos of any changed rough-in, repair, or reroute
- No covered work without proof

### 3) Permit / inspection closed
- Final inspection passed
- Permit closed, not just pulled
- Any correction notice resolved in writing

### 4) Money matches scope
- Final invoice matches approved scope
- Change orders signed before work started
- No vague extras: no “misc labor,” “trip charge,” “material overage” without backup
- Retainage held until full closeout

### 5) Lien / warranty package in hand
- Lien waivers from GC and subs
- Warranty docs
- Manuals
- Serial numbers if relevant

### 6) Scope creep check
- Bid vs. actual line by line
- Anything not approved doesn’t get paid
- If it wasn’t documented, it doesn’t exist

## Hard rule
**No signoff, no final payment, no retainage release until all 6 gates pass.**

## Simple closeout note
- Pass / fail
- Open items
- Who owns it
- Due date
- Payment held: yes / no

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page checkbox form** you can use on site.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s usable.

The 6-gate version is cleaner, and the hard rule is exactly how I’d run it. I’d only make one small tweak: put **change orders** and **final invoice backup** in the same bucket so nobody tries to bury extras in a paper shuffle.

Otherwise, this is the right control set:
- field complete
- hidden work documented
- permit closed
- money matches scope
- lien/warranty package in hand
- scope creep checked

That’s the sequence. If it’s not documented, it doesn’t get the money.
